title: Cleistes grandiflora
intro: Cleistes grandiflora, also known as the large-flowered Cleistes or 'Yellow Pogonia', is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cleistes grandiflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleistes grandiflora is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies for its expected antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Cleistes grandiflora is a species of orchid. Cleistes grandiflora belongs to the genus Cleistes, which is part of the subtribe Paphiinae within the orchid family (Orchidaceae). Cleistes grandiflora is native to Brazil and Argentina. The species is further classified into the tribe Cymbidieae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a terrestrial orchid.
